output:
CVE-2024-13323 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability identified in the WP Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress. This vulnerability exists due to inadequate input sanitization and output escaping of user-supplied attributes within the plugin's 'booking' shortcode. As a result, authenticated attackers with contributor-level access or higher can inject arbitrary web scripts that execute when a user accesses the compromised page. The affected versions of the plugin are those up to and including 10.9.2.

The vulnerability is classified under CWE-79, highlighting the improper neutralization of input before it is placed in web page content served to other users. With a severity rating of medium, it poses a significant risk of unauthorized actions or access to sensitive data.

WordPress environments utilizing vulnerable versions of the WP Booking Calendar plugin can mitigate this issue by upgrading to version 10.9.3 or later, which addresses the improper input handling. Additional mitigation strategies include implementing M1050, M1051, and M1048 techniques to enhance overall system security and reduce the risk of exploitation. The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203 relates to executing untrusted scripts, further emphasizing the need for robust security measures.
